free_text: High purity natural cobalt foils 10 um from Goodfellow.
Al monitor foils (100 um, 40-MeV experiment) and
Ti foils (12 um, 20 and 10 MeV experiments) to follow
the energy degradation along the stack.
The stack also contained Ta (25 um) and Sn (7 um) foil
to measure cross-sections on these materials
Deuteron beams of 40, 20 and 10 MeV between 100 and
200 nA beam intensities for 0.5-2.5 h  were used.
Targets were irradiated in Faraday-cup type holder.
Measurements started from 1 h  after EOB.
The beam intensities obtained via charge collection
were corrected with the data from the monitor
The energy degradation was initially determined by
calculation using the formulas and tables of Andersen
and Ziegler and corrected on the basis of the
The nuclear data were taken from the NUDAT2 database.
Taking into account the cumulative effects of
possible incident energy variation and thickness of
the different targets, the uncertainty of the energy
of the front foil of a stack is around 0.3 MeV and
increases up to 0.5-1.2 MeV for the last foil of the
The estimated total uncertainty on the cross-
sections was about 10% by taking the square root of
the sum in quadrature of individual contributions,
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model code ALICE, EMPIRE
Fitted excitation functions were used for
